summary: Drew DeVault excoriates Elastic for relicensing Elasticsearch away from open source, arguing this betrays the 1,573 contributors who donated their work under an open source social contract. He accuses Elastic of deliberately using CLAs to strip contributors of control, then exploiting that power to establish a monopoly. DeVault rejects Elastic's framing that Amazon forced their hand, noting Elastic cleared half a billion dollars. He argues open source is a communal commitment with moral obligations, and calls this an 'Oracle-level move' against the community.
key_points: Elastic exploited contributor license agreements (CLAs) to relicense community-contributed code, a strategy DeVault argues was intentional from the start
The relicensing betrays 1,573 contributors who believed they were participating in open source, including Elastic's own employees
Elastic's financial success ($500M+ revenue) undermines their claim that Amazon was threatening their survival
Open source carries a moral obligation: once you enter work into the commons, the community collectively owns it
CLAs are dangerous tools that enable exactly this kind of corporate betrayal of community trust
money_quotes: Elastic has spit in the face of every single one of 1,573 contributors, and everyone who gave Elastic their trust, loyalty, and patronage.
Elastic was not having their lunch eaten by Amazon. They cleared half a billion dollars last year. Don't gaslight us.
You don't own it. Everyone owns it. This is why open source is valuable.
If you want to play on the FOSS playing field, then you play by the goddamn rules.
Open source is a community endeavour. It's a committment to enter your work into the commons, and to allow the community to collectively benefit from it — even financially.
I hope everyone reading will remember this as yet another lesson in the art of never signing a CLA.
key_insight: Open source licensing is a moral commitment to the commons, and companies that use CLAs to later revoke that commitment are betraying every contributor who trusted them.
